# KEYSTONE SOCIAL MEDIA INFRASTRUCTURE MASTER REFERENCE
<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / The Canonical Agent Guide — Updated 2026-05-22 -->
## The Canonical Agent Guide — Updated 2026-05-22

> **PURPOSE**: This is the **single source of truth** for how all social media accounts, YouTube channels, tokens, and API credentials are structured across the Keystone dual-brand empire. **Every agent** — Flash, Opus, overnight researchers, and subagents — MUST consult this document before performing any social media operation. It supersedes all prior social documentation.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / CRITICAL RULE: ONE EMAIL RULES EVERYTHING -->
## CRITICAL RULE: ONE EMAIL RULES EVERYTHING

**All social platforms are owned by one human: Curtis "Wayne" Stevenson**
**All accounts are registered under one primary email: `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`**

Wayne uses the username `stevenson4vancouver` on LinkedIn.

---

#52603. | keystonepossibilities.com |
| `recomposition` | Keystone Recomposition | B2C Health/wellness, GLP-1 peptide protocols, body recomposition, longevity, music production. | keystonerecomposition.com |
| `protocol` | Keystone Protocols | Peptide science deep-dives, clinical case studies, GLP-1 muscle preservation, biohacking. Shares infrastructure with Recomposition. | (shares recomposition website) |

> **KEY INSIGHT**: `protocol` is NOT a separate infrastructure. It shares all social tokens, YouTube channel, and Meta/TikTok accounts with `recomposition`. The distinction is purely content-editorial — Protocol content is more clinical/scientific while Recomposition is broader wellness+music.

## 2. YOUTUBE CHANNELS (All under `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`)

All three YouTube channels live under the **same Google account**: `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`. Wayne selects the target channel during OAuth consent.

| Channel Name | Channel ID | Brand | Token File | Login Email |
|-------------|-----------|-------|------------|-------------|
| Keystone Possibilities | `UCu8gdU_R8XE2RvcttGa3drg` | `possibilities` | `youtube_token_possibilities.json` | curtis4vancouver@gmail.com |
| KeyStone Recomposition | `UCMn1f9DTF_iybKmv5WlTm9Q` | `recomposition` + `protocol` | `youtube_token_oac.json` | curtis4vancouver@gmail.com |
| Keystone Protocols | `UCxURlqMNhAtxUTpdXmlOYaw` | (DEPRECATED — videos go to Recomposition) | `youtube_token_protocols.json` | curtis4vancouver@gmail.com |

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / YouTube Routing Rules -->
### YouTube Routing Rules
```
possibilities  → uploads to Keystone Possibilities channel
recomposition  → uploads to KeyStone Recomposition channel
protocol       → uploads to KeyStone Recomposition channel (SAME as recomposition!)
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / YouTube Token Map (in youtube_mcp.py) -->

```python
CHANNEL_TOKEN_MAP = {
    "possibilities": "youtube_token_possibilities.json",
    "protocols": "youtube_token_protocols.json",
    "recomposition": "youtube_token_oac.json",
    "oac": "youtube_token_oac.json",
}
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / YouTube OAuth Script -->
### YouTube OAuth Script
```bash
# Authenticate a specific channel:
python scratch/auth_youtube_channels.py --channel possibilities
python scratch/auth_youtube_channels.py --channel recomposition
python scratch/auth_youtube_channels.py --channel protocols
```
All three prompt login via `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`. The user then selects the correct channel in the Google consent screen.

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 3. FACEBOOK & INSTAGRAM (Meta Graph API) -->
## 3. FACEBOOK & INSTAGRAM (Meta Graph API)

Both **Possibilities** and **Recomposition** Facebook Pages and Instagram accounts are owned by **Curtis Stevenson's personal Facebook account** under `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`.

| Brand | Facebook Page | Instagram Account | Token Key in social_tokens.json |
|-------|-------------|-------------------|--------------------------------|
| `possibilities` | Keystone Possibilities | @keystonepossibilities (or similar) | `possibilities.meta.user_access_token` |
| `recomposition` | Keystone Recomposition | @keystonerecomposition (or similar) | `recomposition.meta.user_access_token` |
| `protocol` | **Inherits from Recomposition** | **Inherits from Recomposition** | Falls back to `recomposition.meta.user_access_token` |

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Meta App Credentials -->
### Meta App Credentials
- **App ID**: `1495585472113538`
- **App Name**: Keystone Automator
- **Redirect URI**: `http://localhost:8080/callback`

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Meta Token Lifespan -->
### Meta Token Lifespan
- Short-lived token: ~1 hour
- Long-lived token: **60 days** (stored in `social_tokens.json`)
- Page Access Tokens derived from long-lived UAT are effectively **never-expiring** (as long as the page permissions remain granted)

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Meta OAuth Scripts -->
### Meta OAuth Scripts
```bash
# Authenticate Possibilities Meta:
python social_oauth_manager.py   # (original flow, selects Possibilities page)

# Authenticate Recomposition Meta:
python scratch/auth_recomposition_meta.py   # (forces re-auth, select Recomposition page + IG)
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / How Meta Page Selection Works -->
### How Meta Page Selection Works
During Facebook OAuth login, the user sees a consent screen listing their Facebook Pages. They MUST check the correct page. After authentication:
- Call `GET /me/accounts` with the user_access_token to see which pages are accessible
- Each page returns its own `page_access_token`
- Instagram Business Account is linked to the Facebook Page (retrieved via `/{page_id}?fields=instagram_business_account`)

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 4. TIKTOK -->
## 4. TIKTOK

TikTok uses a **single account** for all brands under `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com`. The token is stored under the `recomposition` brand but is shared with `possibilities` and `protocol` via fallback logic.

| Brand | TikTok Account | Token Source |
|-------|---------------|-------------|
| `recomposition` | Direct — `recomposition.tiktok.access_token` | Primary |
| `possibilities` | **Inherits from Recomposition** | Fallback |
| `protocol` | **Inherits from Recomposition** | Fallback |

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / TikTok App Credentials -->
### TikTok App Credentials
- **Client Key**: `sbawve91fhxu7wf6sw`
- **App Name**: Keystone Syndicator

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / TikTok Token Lifespan -->
### TikTok Token Lifespan
- Access token: **24 hours** (must be refreshed before each publishing session)
- Refresh token: **~1 year**

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / TikTok Token Refresh -->
### TikTok Token Refresh
```bash
python scratch/refresh_tiktok_token.py
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / TikTok OAuth Re-authentication -->
### TikTok OAuth Re-authentication
```bash
python scratch/reauth_tiktok_recomposition.py
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 6. TOKEN INHERITANCE & FALLBACK LOGIC -->
## 6. TOKEN INHERITANCE & FALLBACK LOGIC

The `social_publisher.py` implements automatic credential fallback so that brands without their own tokens can piggyback on shared accounts:

```
┌─────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐
│  POSSIBILITIES  │     │  RECOMPOSITION   │     │   PROTOCOL   │
├─────────────────┤     ├──────────────────┤     ├──────────────┤
│ LinkedIn: OWN   │     │ LinkedIn: OWN    │     │ LinkedIn: ←──┼── inherits from Recomposition
│ Meta:     OWN   │     │ Meta:     OWN    │     │ Meta:     ←──┼── inherits from Recomposition
│ TikTok:   ←─────┼──── │ TikTok:   OWN    │ ──→ │ TikTok:   ←──┼── inherits from Recomposition
│ YouTube:  OWN   │     │ YouTube:  OWN    │     │ YouTube:  ←──┼── routes to Recomposition channel
└─────────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └──────────────┘
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / How Fallback Works in Code -->
### How Fallback Works in Code
In `social_publisher.py`, each `publish_to_*` method checks:
1. Does the brand have its own token for this platform?
2. If NOT, and brand is `protocol` → use `recomposition`'s token
3. If NOT, and brand is `possibilities` and platform is `tiktok` → use `recomposition`'s token

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 7. THE social_tokens.json STRUCTURE -->
## 7. THE social_tokens.json STRUCTURE

Location: `c:\Users\Curtis\New folder\construction-website\Keystone_HQ\00_Master_Brain\social_tokens.json`

```json
{
    "possibilities": {
        "linkedin": { "client_id": "...", "client_secret": "...", "access_token": "...", "expires_in": 5184000, "acquired_at": <unix_ts> },
        "meta": { "app_id": "...", "app_secret": "...", "user_access_token": "...", "expires_in": 5183999, "acquired_at": <unix_ts> }
    },
    "recomposition": {
        "tiktok": { "client_key": "...", "client_secret": "...", "access_token": "...", "refresh_token": "...", "expires_in": 86400, "refresh_expires_in": 31373983, "acquired_at": <unix_ts> },
        "linkedin": { "client_id": "...", "client_secret": "...", "access_token": "...", "expires_in": 5183999, "acquired_at": <unix_ts> },
        "meta": { "app_id": "...", "app_secret": "...", "user_access_token": "...", "expires_in": 5184000, "acquired_at": <unix_ts> }
    }
}
```

> **NOTE**: There is NO `protocol` key in `social_tokens.json`. Protocol always inherits from `recomposition`.

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 8. KEY FILES [[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]|[[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]|[[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]|[[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]] -->
## 8. KEY FILES [[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]|[[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]|[[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]|[[wiki/[[wiki/index|index]]|[[wiki/index|INDEX]]

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `social_tokens.json` | All active social media API tokens |
| `social_publisher.py` | Multi-platform publishing engine with fallback logic |
| `social_oauth_manager.py` | Master OAuth flow for all platforms |
| `youtube_mcp.py` | YouTube MCP server (list, upload, update, privacy) |
| `youtube_api_manager.py` | Low-level YouTube API wrapper |
| `youtube_oauth.py` | Legacy YouTube OAuth (single channel) |
| `youtube_token.json` | Legacy primary YouTube token (deprecated for multi-channel) |
| `youtube_token_possibilities.json` | Possibilities channel OAuth token |
| `youtube_token_oac.json` | Recomposition channel OAuth token |
| `youtube_token_protocols.json` | Protocols channel OAuth token (rarely needed) |
| `scratch/auth_youtube_channels.py` | Multi-channel YouTube authentication |
| `scratch/auth_recomposition_meta.py` | Recomposition-specific Meta OAuth |
| `scratch/auth_recomposition_linkedin.py` | Recomposition-specific LinkedIn OAuth |
| `scratch/reauth_tiktok_recomposition.py` | TikTok re-authentication flow |
| `scratch/refresh_tiktok_token.py` | TikTok token refresh (24hr cycle) |
| `content_queue.json` | 2-week content staging queue |

---

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 9. AGENT OPERATING PROCEDURES -->
## 9. AGENT OPERATING PROCEDURES

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Before Any Upload or Publish -->
### Before Any Upload or Publish
1. **Read this document** to understand account structure
2. **Check `social_tokens.json`** — verify the target brand has valid tokens (or valid fallback)
3. **Check token age** — TikTok expires every 24 hours; Meta and LinkedIn last 60 days
4. **Verify YouTube channel** — if uploading video, ALWAYS verify the authenticated channel title matches the target before uploading

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Token Expiry Thresholds -->
### Token Expiry Thresholds
| Platform | Access Token TTL | Refresh Token TTL | Auto-Refresh Available? |
|----------|-----------------|-------------------|------------------------|
| YouTube | ~1 hour (auto-refreshes via google-auth library) | Long-lived | Yes, automatic |
| TikTok | 24 hours | ~1 year | Yes, via `refresh_tiktok_token.py` |
| Meta | 60 days (long-lived) | N/A (re-auth needed) | No |
| LinkedIn | 60 days | N/A (re-auth needed) | No |

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Error Prevention Rules (from correction_journal.json) -->
### Error Prevention Rules (from correction_journal.json)
1. **CJ-001**: ALWAYS verify YouTube channel title before upload. Never assume token = correct channel.
2. **CJ-002**: TikTok needs `video.publish` scope (not just `video.upload`) for direct posting.
3. **CJ-003**: After Meta OAuth, verify `/me/accounts` returns the expected pages. If missing, re-auth.
4. **CJ-004**: On Windows, ALWAYS add `sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding='utf-8')` and avoid emoji in prints.
5. **CJ-005**: Refresh TikTok tokens before any publish operation. They expire every 24 hours.
6. **CJ-006**: Use case-insensitive comparison when matching playlist names.

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / Publishing Commands -->
### Publishing Commands
```bash
# Check queue status:
python social_publisher.py --status

# Process all due posts:
python social_publisher.py --process

# Dry-run (no actual publishing):
python social_publisher.py --process --dry-run

# Enqueue a new post:
python social_publisher.py --enqueue --brand recomposition --platforms linkedin,facebook,instagram --content "Your post text"
```

<!-- CONTEXT: Social Media Infrastructure Master / 11. QUICK REFERENCE CHEAT SHEET -->
## 11. QUICK REFERENCE CHEAT SHEET

| Question | Answer |
|----------|--------|
| What email owns everything? | `curtis4vancouver@gmail.com` |
| How many YouTube channels? | 3 (Possibilities, Recomposition, Protocols) — but Protocol routes to Recomposition |
| How many Facebook pages? | 2 (Possibilities, Recomposition) — Protocol shares Recomposition |
| How many TikTok accounts? | 1 — shared across all brands |
| How many LinkedIn accounts? | 1 personal profile — shared across brands |
| Where are tokens stored? | `social_tokens.json` in Master Brain root |
| Where are YouTube tokens? | `youtube_token_*.json` files in Master Brain root |
| What's the OAuth callback? | `http://localhost:8080/callback` (Meta/TikTok/LinkedIn) or `http://localhost:8080/` (YouTube) |
| Does Protocol have its own tokens? | NO — always inherits from Recomposition |
| What needs daily refresh? | TikTok access token (24hr) via `refresh_tiktok_token.py` |
